steps.title: Identify what we need for sin R
steps.content: To find $\sin R$, we need the ratio $\frac{\text{opposite}}{\text{hypotenuse}} = \frac{TS}{TR}$. We know the hypotenuse TR = 53 and one leg RS = \sqrt{105}. Use the Pythagorean theorem to find the other leg, TS.

steps.title: Calculate TS, then find sin R
steps.content:
With hypotenuse TR = 53 and leg RS = \sqrt{105}:
$$53^2 = (\sqrt{105})^2 + TS^2$$
$$2809 = 105 + TS^2$$
$$TS^2 = 2704$$
$$TS = 52$$

The side opposite angle R is TS = 52 and the hypotenuse is TR = 53, so $\sin R = \frac{52}{53}$.
